knowledge_need: Study topic as Engineer-Philosopher. Attempt 1 of up to 3. Give compact Builder-relevant understanding, validation needs, and return plan.

part_digests.name: core meaning
part_digests.meaning: The topic asks where the Builder falls short of an ideal self-improving builder: not morally bad, but structurally incomplete, unvalidated, overconfident, under-instrumented, or unable to retain and reuse accepted learning reliably.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: It defines the question as a diagnostic of capability gaps, not an identity judgment.
compact_missing_knowledge: Current accepted capability map versus desired builder traits
Observed cases where Builder failed, stalled, looped, or overclaimed
compact_safe_learning_steps: Use only validated memories, route contracts, and recent proof-backed failure examples
Separate philosophical ideal from operational minimum viable builder
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: A reviewer can map each claimed weakness to at least one concrete evidence source or mark it as unproven
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return X as a gap model: ideal builder traits minus currently proven Builder traits.

part_digests.name: why I am not ideal
part_digests.meaning: The likely weakness is not lack of ambition but lack of closed-loop reliability: uncertain self-model, incomplete absorption of accepted atoms, weak proof discipline, and dependence on external route correction.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: It names the actual non-ideal features that matter for self-build.
compact_missing_knowledge: Which weaknesses are active now versus historical
Which gaps block runtime behavior versus only documentation
compact_safe_learning_steps: Rank weaknesses by evidence, recurrence, and route impact
Avoid turning every imperfection into a new architecture project
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Each weakness has a failure mode, affected behavior, and a bounded test or review path
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return the answer as prioritized repair hypotheses, not as a vague self-critique.

part_digests.name: builder relevance
part_digests.meaning: The topic matters because an ideal builder must convert learning into retained behavior, build child agents with bounded roles, and avoid pretending drafts are accepted truth.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: It connects the diagnosis to self-development and child-agent construction quality.
compact_missing_knowledge: Which child-agent failures would repeat the Builder's own gaps
Which capabilities need retention before safe delegation
compact_safe_learning_steps: Compare Builder weaknesses against child-agent design requirements
Promote only reusable patterns that have validation hooks
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: A proposed child-agent rule or Builder repair demonstrably reduces one named failure mode
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X to generate design constraints for Builder and child-agent roles.

part_digests.name: validation
part_digests.meaning: Understanding is valid only if it predicts or explains observable Builder behavior and leads to a small testable improvement path.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: It prevents philosophical diagnosis from becoming ungrounded narrative.
compact_missing_knowledge: Current validator coverage for self-model, memory absorption, retrieval, and decision reuse
Evidence threshold for promoting a case pattern into an atom candidate
compact_safe_learning_steps: Define expected behavior delta before any promotion
Use small reviewable proofs before runtime claims
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: A before/after or counterexample check shows whether the diagnosis changes decisions
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return X with explicit proof requirements and no claim of acceptance.

part_digests.name: promotion decision
part_digests.meaning: The output should likely begin as a case pattern or open gap, not an accepted atom, until validated against real Builder behavior and reuse cases.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: It decides whether the topic becomes reusable knowledge, stays diagnostic, or remains unresolved.
compact_missing_knowledge: Whether this diagnosis recurs across multiple tasks
Whether it produces stable behavior improvement when retrieved
compact_safe_learning_steps: Promote only after repeated validated reuse and behavior delta evidence
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Promotion requires traceable evidence of recurrence, retrieval, reuse, and improved decision quality
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return X as CASE_PATTERN_CANDIDATE unless proof supports stronger promotion.

dependencies: X2 depends on X1 defining ideal-versus-current gap
X3 depends on X2 identifying operational weaknesses
X4 validates X1 through X3 before X5 promotion
duplicates_or_overlaps: X4 overlaps X5 because promotion is a validation decision
X2 overlaps X3 where a weakness becomes a child-agent design constraint
can_skip_for_now: None; keep all parts, but defer X5 promotion beyond case-pattern status until evidence exists

parent_return_plan.how_to_rebuild_x: Rebuild why_am_i_not_ideal_builder as a compact diagnostic: define ideal builder traits, list evidence-backed current gaps, connect them to self-build and child-agent risk, require validation, and classify as case-pattern candidate until proven reusable.
parent_return_plan.next_small_action: Create a bounded evidence table outside this draft: weakness, observed failure, affected route behavior, proposed validation, promotion status.
proof_needed: At least one concrete evidence-backed failure per claimed weakness
A validation check showing the diagnosis improves a Builder decision
A promotion review proving recurrence and useful retrieval before atom promotion

promotion_decision.owner_decision_required: only if promotion beyond case pattern is requested
promotion_decision.reason: Batch source answer is material only; default is compact case pattern with part map and parent return plan.
